text:
*[Bug 18995] New: tre-parse.c has a variety of out-of-bounds reads*
<https://bugs.r-project.org/show_bug.cgi?id=18995>

            Bug ID: 18995
           Summary: tre-parse.c has a variety of out-of-bounds reads
           Product: R
           Version: R-devel (trunk)
          Hardware: Other
                OS: Linux
            Status: UNCONFIRMED
          Severity: normal
          Priority: P5
         Component: Wishlist
          Assignee: <mailto:R-core@R-project.org|R-core@R-project.org>
          Reporter: <mailto:kevinushey@gmail.com|kevinushey@gmail.com>

Created attachment 3601
  --> <https://bugs.r-project.org/attachment.cgi?id=3601&action=edit>
Candidate patch.

These can usually be triggered using `grepRaw()` with invalid or incomplete
patterns with R build with ASAN. For example:

```
> grepRaw("(", "abc")
=================================================================
==1871632==ERROR: AddressSanitizer: heap-buffer-overflow on address
0x7b30fd9f99b4 at pc 0x7f1102f9db0e bp 0x7fffca3dab30 sp 0x7fffca3dab28
READ of size 4 at 0x7b30fd9f99b4 thread T0

/home/kevin/r-devel/trunk/src/extra/tre/tre-parse.c:1227

/home/kevin/r-devel/trunk/src/extra/tre/tre-compile.c:1920
    #2 0x7f1102f60680 in tre_regncompb
/home/kevin/r-devel/trunk/src/extra/tre/regcomp.c:118
    #3 0x7f1102b04e3f in do_grepraw
/home/kevin/r-devel/trunk/src/main/grep.c:1736
    #4 0x7f1102a1a483 in bcEval_loop
/home/kevin/r-devel/trunk/src/main/eval.c:8132
    #5 0x7f1102a6d064 in bcEval /home/kevin/r-devel/trunk/src/main/eval.c:7515
    #6 0x7f1102a6db85 in Rf_eval /home/kevin/r-devel/trunk/src/main/eval.c:1167
    #7 0x7f1102a7b90b in R_execClosure
/home/kevin/r-devel/trunk/src/main/eval.c:2389
    #8 0x7f1102a7bc84 in applyClosure_core
/home/kevin/r-devel/trunk/src/main/eval.c:2302
    #9 0x7f1102a7f46c in Rf_applyClosure
/home/kevin/r-devel/trunk/src/main/eval.c:2324
    #10 0x7f1102a6fafd in Rf_eval
/home/kevin/r-devel/trunk/src/main/eval.c:1280
    #11 0x7f1102b61c45 in Rf_ReplIteration
/home/kevin/r-devel/trunk/src/main/main.c:264
    #12 0x7f1102b621f3 in R_ReplConsole
/home/kevin/r-devel/trunk/src/main/main.c:317
    #13 0x7f1102b6237f in run_Rmainloop
/home/kevin/r-devel/trunk/src/main/main.c:1235
    #14 0x7f1102b623cc in Rf_mainloop
/home/kevin/r-devel/trunk/src/main/main.c:1242
    #15 0x0000004004f3 in main /home/kevin/r-devel/trunk/src/main/Rmain.c:29
    #16 0x7f11018105b4 in __libc_start_call_main (/lib64/libc.so.6+0x35b4)
(BuildId: 57f6900d44d37352280570abdffc4f01a1803226)
    #17 0x7f1101810667 in __libc_start_main@@GLIBC_2.34
(/lib64/libc.so.6+0x3667) (BuildId: 57f6900d44d37352280570abdffc4f01a1803226)
    #18 0x0000004003e4 in _start
(/home/kevin/r/r-devel-sanitizers/lib64/R/bin/exec/R+0x4003e4) (BuildId:
a96b5b8788e93e4b9d0462028242bff17a2aceb1)
```

I've attached a patch for some of the specific access issues I've found.

```
> sessionInfo()
R Under development (unstable) (2026-01-25 r89330)
Platform: x86_64-pc-linux-gnu
Running under: Fedora Linux 43 (Workstation Edition)
```
